mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month PAYEMS increases coincide with negative 20d returns in more than 50 percent of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates the risk-free discount rate and debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 5% episodes produce positive 20d returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive month-over-month change in CPIAUCSL and negative month-over-month change in INDPRO signal stagflationary pressure that raises equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces positive 60d returns in more than 50 percent of instances or the interaction term coefficient is non-negative in a regression, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a rise in the unemployment rate signals weakening aggregate demand and downward earnings revision risk before the central bank pivots, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during periods of FEDFUNDS > 4%, rising UNRATE coincides with positive 20d returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,300 thousand units signal deteriorating residential investment and forward demand weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST < 1300 episodes coincide with positive 60d returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate sales, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5d returns in more than 50 percent of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates broad-based demand strength that boosts corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If joint increases in PAYEMS and RSAFS are followed by negative 20d returns in more than 50 percent of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4.0% signals that labor market slack is emerging, reducing household income growth and consumption forecasts, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE > 4.0% coincides with positive 5d returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.
